item.title: Chinese satellite maker GalaxySpace starts IPO process
item.description: Chinese satellite maker GalaxySpace has begun the so-called tutoring process for a potential initial public offering, ​state media reported on Tuesday, the latest private space firm ‌to take steps towards an IPO as China encourages them to tap the country's capital markets.

item.title: Oil and war top financial markets worry list for an uncertain Q2
item.description: Battered financial markets enter the second quarter significantly exposed to war headlines, a backdrop that could prompt a bigger retreat for equity markets while a hefty selloff in bonds could tempt buyers back.

item.title: BlackRock weighs HSBC's Canary Wharf tower for new London headquarters, FT reports
item.description: BlackRock is considering a Canary Wharf skyscraper that HSBC will vacate ​next year for its London ‌headquarters, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Morning Bid: Some respite for nervy markets after brutal month
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: Japan brands yen falls as 'speculative' as Iran war ignites sell-off
item.description: Japan on Tuesday labelled recent yen falls as speculative for ​the first time since the Middle East war began, shifting its focus back to currency short-sellers as policymakers braced for a triple market sell-off ‌driven by fresh inflationary concerns.

item.title: China's top airlines cautious on outlook as Iran war raises fuel costs
item.description: China's three largest state-owned airlines said they were cautious about the outlook for this year as the Iran war drives ​jet fuel prices sky-high, after all returned to losses in the fourth quarter of 2025.

item.title: Microsoft plans $1 billion investment in Thailand, Thai government says
item.description: Microsoft ​plans to invest $1 ‌billion in Thailand over the next ​two years ​in cloud services and ⁠AI infrastructure, the Thai ​government said in ​a statement.

item.title: US senators question FCC chief over Nexstar-Tegna deal, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The ranking members of the U.S. Senate Commerce Committee questioned ​the Federal Communications Commission Chair Brendan Carr and criticized ‌his approval of Nexstar's merger with Tegna without a vote from the full commission, Bloomberg News reported on Monday.

item.title: California AI order requires firms seeking state contracts to have safeguards against abuse
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom signed an executive order on Monday that requires firms seeking contracts with ​the state to provide safeguards against AI misuse, ‌including the generation of illegal content, harmful bias and violations of civil rights.

item.title: Indonesia summons Meta and Google over non-compliance with child social media curbs, minister says
item.description: Indonesia summoned officials from Meta and Google over non-compliance with social media restrictions for children under 16 that went into full ​effect last week, a minister said in a video statement published ‌on Tuesday.

item.title: Cathay Pacific to maintain capacity despite rising jet fuel costs, CEO says
item.description: Cathay Pacific Airways CEO Ronald Lam ​said on Monday that the airline's short-term ‌priority was to maintain flight capacity, adding that any cutbacks would be a "last resort" even as the ​conflict in the Middle East drives jet ​fuel prices higher.

item.title: Asia shares falter as Iran war rages
item.description: Oil prices on Tuesday were set for a record monthly rise while Asian shares were headed for their steepest fall since 2022, ​capping a tumultuous month as the war in the Middle East stoked fears of higher inflation and slower growth.

item.title: Australia's Collins Foods to transfer 20 Taco Bell outlets to Yum Brands
item.description: Australia's Collins Foods said ​on Tuesday it would hand over 20 of its 27 ‌Taco Bell restaurants in the country to an affiliate of Yum Brands and Restaurant Brands, moving closer to a full exit from the loss-making business.

item.title: Oil rises for a fourth day on supply cuts from widening Middle East conflict
item.description: Iran's effective closure of the Strait of ⁠Hormuz has ​pushed Brent futures up 59% so far in March, its highest monthly gain ever, while WTI is up 58% this month, ​the most since May 2020.

item.title: American Exchange Group to buy sneaker maker Allbirds for $39 million
item.description: American Exchange Group (AXNY) entered an agreement on Monday to buy all ​the assets and liabilities of ‌footwear maker Allbirds for $39 million.

item.title: Australian $240 billion pension fund snaps up Japanese, European stocks and UK bonds
item.description: Australia's No.2 pension fund has ​increased its global equities, Australian and British bond investments, some of the world's most sold-off ‌asset classes, in the past month to take advantage of financial market volatility created by the Iran war.

item.title: China establishes data organisation aimed at global industry consensus
item.description: China has launched the World Data Organisation, a professional body ​to promote orderly data development and ‌utilisation as well as its governance globally, according to state television CCTV.

item.title: Samsung SDI to lend $1.05 billion to Stellantis JV StarPlus Energy
item.description: South Korea's ​Samsung SDI ‌said it plans to ​lend ​1.6 trillion won ($1.05 ⁠billion) to ​StarPlus Energy, ​its battery joint venture with ​Stellantis.

item.title: US pump prices hit $4 a gallon as Iran war wreaks havoc on global energy supply
item.description: The U.S. national average retail ​price of gasoline crossed $4 a gallon for the first time in more than three years on Monday, data ‌from price tracking services GasBuddy showed, as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran continued to roil global energy markets.

item.title: War sets dollar for monthly rise, yen recovers on intervention threat
item.description: The dollar headed for its biggest monthly gain since July on Tuesday and stands out as the strongest so-called safe asset as war ​in the Mideast has set oil prices surging and raised the risk of global recession.

item.title: Australia central bank uncertain on path for rates after March hike
item.description: SYDNEY, March 31 (Reuters) - Australia's central bank board felt monetary policy needed to be restrictive when it decided to raise rates earlier this month in a split decision, although it was ​ready to respond should the conflict in the Middle East lead to an ‌economic downturn.

item.title: American Express named official payments partner of NFL
item.description: American Express has signed ​a multi-year deal to become the National Football League's official payments partner starting ‌with the 2026 season, the company and the league said on Monday.

item.title: Australia investigates tech giants over social media ban compliance
item.description: The Australian internet regulator said it was investigating five of the biggest social media platforms for suspected breaches of its ​new under-16 ban, its strongest signal yet that companies ‌may face enforcement action under a world-first regime.

item.title: Australia to ban surcharging on payment cards, deliver savings worth A$2.5 bln
item.description: SYDNEY, March 31 (Reuters) - Australia's central bank said on Tuesday that it would end surcharges on debit and credit card ​payments for consumers from October 1, while lowering ‌interchange fees paid by businesses in steps estimated to save about A$2.5 billion a year.

item.title: Brazil's Natura announces stake purchase by Advent as founders exit board
item.description: Brazilian cosmetics maker Natura said on Monday ​that U.S. private equity firm ‌Advent has signed a binding agreement to buy a stake in the company, while also ​announcing a board reshuffle that ​includes the departure of its founders.

item.title: Unilever-McCormick potential food unit deal includes $16 billion cash component, WSJ reports
item.description: Unilever ​is in advanced talks to ‌combine its food business with U.S. spice maker McCormick , in a deal ​that includes a cash ​component of around $16 billion, the Wall ⁠Street Journal reported on Monday, ​citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Exclusive: Unilever imposes global hiring freeze, citing Middle East war effects, memo says
item.description: Dove soap maker Unilever has implemented a global ​hiring freeze "at all levels" that will last at least three months, citing the effects ‌of the widening conflict in the Middle East, according to a memo seen by Reuters.

item.title: IMAX says CEO taking medical leave
item.description: IMAX said on Monday that CEO Richard Gelfond, ​who is being treated for ‌pneumonia, was taking a temporary medical leave.

item.title: GM idles Detroit EV plant, temporarily laying off 1,300
item.description: General Motors ​is idling ‌a Detroit electric vehicle ​plant until ​April 13, extending ⁠downtime that ​began on ​March 16, the company said ​on Monday.

item.title: TRADING DAY Growth fears snowball
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit seven-month lows and bond yields fell on Monday as the Iran war entered its fifth week, with investors increasingly spooked by growth fears over inflation concerns even though oil prices rose further above $100 a barrel.
